reforms.description: The government initiated the Secondary/Tertiary Alignment Resource (STAR) to provide opportunities for senior secondary students to access tertiary-level courses, aiming to bridge the gap between secondary and tertiary education.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By enhancing educational pathways, STAR aims to improve the qualifications of the workforce, thereby supporting productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The STAR initiative was put in place to facilitate access to tertiary education for secondary students.
reforms.importance_rationale: The STAR initiative represents a significant effort to improve educational outcomes and align secondary education with tertiary opportunities, which is crucial for enhancing human capital.
reforms.source_quote: In 1996, the Secondary/Tertiary Alignment Resource (STAR) was put in place, providing opportunities for around one-third of senior secondary school students to access courses and programmes at tertiary level.
